series.name: 3M Die-Cuttable Tape Series DC2000
series.product_description: 3M Die-Cuttable Tape Series DC2000 is a black foam tape designed for the attachment of nameplates, badges and emblems to the exterior of vehicles. It is designed to process easily in die-cutting operations, including high-speed rotary operations, and exhibits high resistance to pluck and torque (twisting) forces associated with attachment of nameplates and emblems.
product_features: Excellent adhesion to paint and chrome
Firm core resists twisting forces that may result in detachment of individual letters
Excellent resistance to impact in cold weather
Processing compatibility with rotary, flat bed, and laser die cutting operations
main_applications: Low demand applications whose primary attachment methods are mechanical
Spoiler applications that require tape to perform as a gasket

params.name: Pluck Testing
params.method_html: Typical results. Maintains pluck adhesion on new low surface energy and scratch- and mar-resistant paint systems in addition to conventional paint systems.

params.name: Torque Testing
params.method_html: Typical results. 3M Series DC2000 tape demonstrates higher resistance to twisting forces (torque) compared to typical competitive tapes.

params.name: Static Shear at 70 C
params.method_html: Typical results. Static shear tests are run with 25.4 mm (1.0 in.) wide by 25.4 mm (1 x 1 in.) long tape on chrome, with 6.8 kg (15 lb) roll down against enamel paint. Weight is 500 grams (1.1 lb).

params.name: Shelf Life and Storage
params.method_html: One year from date of receipt by customer when stored at 4 C to 38 C (40 F to 100 F) and 0 to 95% relative humidity. Optimum storage conditions are 22 C (72 F) and 50% RH.

spec_values.test_condition: Stored at 4 C to 38 C and 0 to 95% RH; optimum 22 C and 50% RH
